name: Joyce's Steak and Mushroom Gravy
recipeIngredient: Good round steak, trimmed and cut into serving portions
recipeInstructions:
1. Season both sides of the steak pieces with salt and pepper.
2. Roll each piece thoroughly in flour, coating both sides well.
3. Heat oil or shortening in a skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Add the seasoned steaks to the hot oil and fry until browned on all sides (do not cook through).
5. Remove the steaks from the skillet and set aside.
6. Open at least two cans of mushroom soup and transfer it to a bowl.
7. Stir the soup to smooth out any lumps, then gradually add equal amounts of water, stirring constantly to maintain a smooth consistency.
8. Add milk and/or water if desired (but not too much).
9. Return the steaks to the skillet with the mushroom soup mixture.
10. Bring the pot to a boil, then cover and simmer for at least 1-2 hours or longer until the steak is tender enough to cut with a fork (thicker pieces require more cooking time).
11. The sauce will thicken into gravy as it simmers; continue to cook until desired consistency is reached.
12. Serve the steak with buttery mashed potatoes, fresh green beans, and salad.
Suggestions:
Even people who usually dislike mushrooms enjoy this dish!
In fact, if you don't mention the mushrooms, they likely won't even notice them.
